acara_description: Describe and interpret graphs of real-world situations; identify key features including gradient, intercepts and shape.

questions.stem: A distance–time graph shows a horizontal flat line. What does this tell us about the object?
options: It is moving at constant speed

default: In a distance–time graph, the y-axis shows distance from start.
If the line is horizontal, the distance is not changing.
No change in distance means the object is not moving.

questions.worked_solution: A horizontal line in a distance–time graph means distance is constant — the object is stationary.

questions.stem: A travel graph is steeper at the start than at the end. What does this mean?
options: Speed was slower at the start
Speed was faster at the start

answer.correct: Speed was faster at the start

default: In a distance–time graph, steeper slope = faster speed.
Steeper at start → moving faster at the start.
Less steep at end → slowing down.

questions.worked_solution: Steeper gradient = greater speed. Steeper at start means faster speed at the start.

questions.stem: A temperature graph shows the line going up steeply, then becoming flat. What does the flat section show?

default: A flat line means no change in the quantity.
If temperature is constant, the flat section shows no increase or decrease.
A horizontal line always means no change in the y-value.

questions.worked_solution: A flat section means the temperature stopped changing — it is constant.

questions.stem: A distance–time graph shows a car at 0 km at t = 0 and at 120 km at t = 2 hours. What is the average speed in km/h?

default: Speed = \(\frac{\text{distance}}{\text{time}}\).
Distance = 120 km, time = 2 h.

questions.stem: A graph shows the cost C (in dollars) of hiring a bike: it starts at $5 (the hire fee) and increases by $3 per hour. Which statement is correct?
options: The cost increases by $5 each hour

default: The starting point of the graph (at 0 hours) shows the initial cost.
The graph starts at $5, meaning the hire fee before any hours is $5.
The increase per hour ($3) is the gradient.

questions.worked_solution: The y-intercept ($5 at 0 hours) is the fixed hire fee. The gradient ($3/hour) is the rate.

questions.stem: A cost formula is \(C=3n+5\). What is \(C\) when \(n=10\)?

default: Substitute \(n=10\).

questions.stem: In a distance–time graph, a downward sloping line means:
options: Object is moving back towards the starting point
Object is moving at constant speed

answer.correct: Object is moving back towards the starting point

default: Going down means distance from start is decreasing.
If the object started further away, it must be moving closer to the origin.
Decreasing distance from start = moving back.

questions.worked_solution: A downward slope in a distance–time graph means the object is moving back towards its starting point.

questions.stem: A graph shows two lines representing two cars leaving the same point. Car A has a steeper line than Car B. What does this mean?

default: In a distance–time graph, gradient = speed.
Steeper gradient = greater speed.
Car A has a steeper slope, so it covers more distance per unit time.

questions.worked_solution: Steeper gradient = higher speed. Car A is faster.

questions.stem: A graph of water in a tank shows it filling at a constant rate, then the rate doubles. What does the graph look like?
options: A flat line then a steeper line
A straight line then a horizontal line
A steeper line then a less steep line

answer.correct: A flat line then a steeper line

default: Constant rate = constant gradient = straight line.
Doubling the rate means the gradient doubles.
Second section will be steeper.

questions.worked_solution: Constant fill rate = straight line; doubling the rate = steeper straight line in the second section.

questions.stem: A graph shows: at x = 0, y = 4; at x = 2, y = 10. Assuming a straight-line relationship, what is y when x = 3?

default: Find the gradient (rate of change).
Rise = 10 − 4 = 6 over run = 2. Gradient = 3.
Starting at y=4, add 3 per unit of x. At x=3: 4 + 3×3 = ?
